ubiquitous_language.definition: A record representing physical property data including images and 3D assets (Splats), managed across ingestion and mobile capture contexts.
A structured index entry describing uploaded assets, including format validation (PNG, JPG, GLTF) and storage location.
A temporary storage container for mobile-captured data held locally until network reconnection enables synchronization.
A restricted category of service providers and categories limited to home real property services, excluding general contractors.
A searchable record containing provider service capabilities, location, and relevance score calculated by the AI Agent.
A service quote entry used to render comparison views, requiring minimum three entries for display.
A calculated relevance metric indicating provider quality, used to rank top 5 results within 300ms.
An extracted text segment from CC&R or Bylaws documents with a confidence score greater than 90% highlighting relevant sections.
A calculated time limit for regulatory obligations, triggering alerts 30 days prior to the deadline date.
A regulatory rule set defining mandatory communication content and timing based on local jurisdiction.
A metric indicating the AI's certainty level in CC&R/Bylaws clause extraction.
A unit of work requiring a specific workflow state transition, recorded in the versioned audit log.
A discrete status value representing progress of an ActionItem (e.g., Pending, In-Progress, Complete).
An immutable audit record tracking state changes with 100ms timestamp precision.
A collection of email/SMS payloads processed together, tracking delivery status for bulk communication channels.
A status record of a specific message transmission attempt, including bounce rate and retry logic.
A scoped collection of tenant members targeted for a specific regulatory or service message.
A row-level security configuration enforcing data isolation between multi-tenant environments.
The logical data scope accessible by a specific tenant entity within the multi-tenant database.
A permission tier defining what data an authenticated Homeowner can view or modify.
A request to process deposits or service fees via Stripe/Square, returning authorization within 5 seconds.
A generated electronic proof of payment sent to the Homeowner upon successful authorization.
A specific financial transaction line item associated with service engagement or system usage.
